title: erodium glandulosum subsp. paularense
intro: Erodium glandulosum subsp. paularense, also known as Paularena's heron's bill, is a subspecies of the Erodium glandulosum plant, belonging to the Geraniaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Erodium glandulosum subsp. paularense include reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antiviral and antifungal properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
